entry.title: No junk dimension columns found for a trf table for a cs table in SAP BODS for datahub
entry.content: This is regarding an error being faced in Guidewire Datahub framework where upon running the DH Initial Load it is failing at ODS citing the subject mentioned error. Table names in question are TRF Policy and CS Organization DIM, TRF Prem Trans and CS Product Dim. Datahub code implementation version 9.1 and SAP BODS version 4.3

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam at Gent (Belgium) - Build AI services using the SAP Cloud Application Programming Mo

Thank you. We have received your request and it is now in our planning board. The topic expert will be in touch soon.

Eberechukwu

entry.title: Re: Project Glow: New Design Coming to SAP Community on Monday, June 2
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi - yes, we can, and would love to, help more, with closing questions with unaccepted correct answers. Please alert the moderators by using the "Report Inappropriate Content" (we are working on changing the name to something friendlier), choose "Something else", and let us know in the details field that the answer is correct and can be accepted as a solution. 

Please also leave a comment to the author, if you have time of course, when you see unaccepted answers. I do whenever I come across questions that should have been closed.

Best regards,

Eberechukwu

entry.title: Re: Health Monitoring in SAP Datasphere
entry.content: There is also SAP supportability tools in VS Code as an option.

entry.title: Re: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content: Hello @Gabor5, The "Not applicable" status indicates that the bank account has not been reconciled and currently no bank statement flows are available for reconciliation (for example when the bank statement is not uploaded). For more information, please see: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/eb100328e8464540b2616356eec2f62f.html?locale=en-US

entry.title: Re: SAP CodeJam at Hicron (Wroclaw, Poland)
entry.content: Great 🙂 Thank you!

entry.title: Free Tier BTP Global Account needed for AI Core Instance ?

and i have to install AI Core Instance

https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/btp-free-tier-account.html

but im not sure if it applies for me since i am employee of sap, not customer of sap

BR,

Ridzuan

entry.title: Scenario Datasphere and SAC Story, dynamic valuelist + restricted keyfigure
entry.content:
hi,

we have the following usecase =>

Datasphere Analytical Datamodel

 - Invoice data

Story in SAC: (not an SAC Application)

  -  1 Inputcontrol: Fiscal Period (all periods from current Fiscal year + all periode from previous fiscalyear in the valuelist)

   - 1 table: with Keyfigure turnover (Selected Fiscal Period) 

my questions:

a.)  how to get the periods for the SAC Inputcontrol valuelist? in Stories not possible anmyore to make coding before starting like in  apps, with the "on startup" event?

b.) how to pass the selected Fiscalperiod to DSP that the calcaulation will be done in Datasphere?

     -  with  restricted keyfigure Turnover with restricted variable on Fiscal period ?  

thanks for hints

entry.title: Re: Links to SAP demo recordings for HCM Business AI features
entry.content: This is fantastic! That you very much for sharing, Xavier. Super useful! 🙂

entry.title: Re: Current Preparation Plan for C_S4CFI
entry.content:
Important News - please note that identifying the certification relevant content for C_S4CFI_2404 has now become much easier.

The certification along with all certification relevant courses are now available IN ONE PLACE, in this leaning journey:
Implementing Financial Accounting in SAP S/4HANA Cloud. 

This does not invalidate any of your former preparation efforts, it is just a new assembly of the same content.

entry.title: ISO code P1 is not assigned to a currency key in the valid time period
entry.content:
During migrating the object "Condition record for pricing (general template)" using migration cockpit, we are facing following error:

 

"ISO code P1 is not assigned to a currency key in the valid time period" for all our records in the Simulation Phase.

How to resolve this?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: ISO code P1 is not assigned to a currency key in the...
entry.content:
This error is usually faced when the data filling in sheet / structure for scales is improper.

To resolve this follow this KBA: 3564661 - Data Migration: Conditions with error B1882 "ISO code P1 is not assigned to a currency key in the valid time period" - SAP for Me

Regards

Thanks for sharing your insight. 🙂 

Thanks,

Cat

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA BP screen enhancement with custom tab
entry.content:
Hi Walter,

good question. I checked again with "my" field KNA1-ZCUST and changed the value. Checking change log I can't see any additional field change. Just a guess. It might be that there are some fields which are filled automatically. You can set a breakpoint in BAdI CVI_CUSTOM_MAPPER and check at which fields of structure DATAX is set and analyse afterwards where this X is set.

Best Regards
Andi

entry.title: Re: BGPF SBGQC queue context and destination not transpor...

Dear @Molle @Andre_Fischer . Sorry for reaching out directly; Is this something currently known with BGPF ?

entry.title: Re: What to investigate before deleting users
How to see dimension with associations in datasphere without using analytics?
entry.content:
Hi all

I have a simple dimension built in datasphere and it has some associations, text and others and I want to check the data with the associations without having to build an Analytic model over it

The semantic type of the view in Dimension and is it exposed for consumption

Any ideas?

 

Shlomi

entry.title: Preparation for setting up integration between ECC or S/4HANA OnPrem and Concur
entry.content:
The standard SAP Integration with Concur Solutions (SAP ICS) makes it really easy to quickly setup and configure an integration between your ECC or S/4HANA OnPrem system and SAP Concur. Our Concur Service Delivery team have helped many clients in getting this setup, but there are certain issues we often see clients run into. Fortunately, there are a few easy checks and guidelines you can follow to avoid them.

The purpose of this article is to give a quick and simple overview of the guidelines to ensure you are best prepared for a smooth integration. 

Add-ons:

For on-premise integration, it is essential that the latest service packs (SAP Note 2298170) are installed. You can find these in the Master Guide on the SAP Help Portal.

The following add-ons must be downloaded and installed for SAP ECC and SAP S/4 HANA On-Prem 1511-1909:

  • CTE_FND 100 – Foundation Component (always required)
  • CTE_HCM 100 – HCM Integration (optional, for the replication of employee master data and payroll / Payroll)
  • CTE_FIN 100 – Financial Integration (required for FI postings and the replication of cost object types)

Installation instructions for installing the add-ons using SAINT can be found in SAP Note 2298170 under sections 3 and 4.
Other add-ons should not be installed, only the ones listed above are required.
Assign authorizations and AuthProfiles according to the MasterGuide.

Importing Certificates:

Unlock the firewall and import the certificates in STRUST under SSL Client Standard.

  • DigiCert Global Root CA
  • DigiCert Global Root G2
  • DigiCert Global Root G3

The certificates can be obtained from the DigiCert website:
www.digicert.com/digicert-root-certificates.htm

Enable Communication (Firewall):

The following addresses must be allowed in the firewall for outbound communication via REST API:

EMEA datacenter

  • eu2.concursolutions.com
  • eu2.api.concursolutions.com

US datacenter

  • us2.concursolutions.com
  • us2.api.concursolutions.com

Detailed information on this, as well as on integration via middleware (if necessary), can be found in SAP Note 2914977 - FAQ: Concur Certificates, Authentication, and Connectivity.

For customers with SAP integration platforms, we recommend the following documentation in the SAP Business Accelerator:  https://api.sap.com/package/ConcurIntegrationDataExportandFIPosting/integrationflow

Next Steps:

  • Review the settings of the Crypto Library and Cipher Suite (SAP Note  2914977)

entry.title: Re: What to investigate before deleting users
entry.content: Thanks George, I already do this this as part of my general termination process. I was more thinking of techical consequences. I'm trying to figure of if there could be any consequences of deleting the communication user.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Add Subaccount to Cloud Connector – Certifi...
entry.content: Thanks a lot, HAL9000! Your suggestion to update the JDK fixed the certificate issue in Cloud Connector. Really appreciate your help!

entry.title: Prevention of charm approval procedure
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

We are having scenario where CHARM Change Control request for change was approved by the same user (Application responsible role) bypassing the established change management process. The approval was granted without the required Process owner (PO), CSA (Computer System Assurance) and DQ (Digital Quality) review and sign-off. Attaching screenshot for your reference. 

Kindly let us know how this can be prevented in the future, whether role modification is required or any charm configuration will prevent this to happen in the future.

SAP Solution Manager SOLMAN Change Control Management  @DoloresCorrea @Richard_Büffing

entry.title: Re: Monetization Secrets: How Top SAP Developers Earn $15K/Month From Apps
entry.content:
@rajesh_munusamyAccording to our Rules of Engagement, I have added the "GenAI Assisted Content" user tag to your post, as the content has been AI-generated. Transparency is key when it comes to AI, we therefore require that AI-generated content is marked as such: "If you want to publish content that was helped/created by GenAI, you must add the user tag GenAI Assisted Content. For content where it's not possible to add a user tag (e.g., answers), you must include this statement: "GenAI was used to help generate this content."

Furthermore, please edit your post and use the "Insert/edit code sample" functionality of our blogging editor for your code samples. You can find it in the toolbar on top of the editor. It will make it easier for your readers to read the code, as well as copy.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to index variant attributes to find them from search
entry.content:
Adding ISO code is work around.
You don't need to change composed type. Change Indexer queries to get variants.

entry.title: Longtext missing - SAP Plant Maintenance
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

In SAP Plant Maintenance in the maintenance order operation the longtext is not showing for another languages. I added the text with user language DE. When working with another language (FR, EN) the text is showing in the order but not in the maintenance scheduling board. Do I have to translate this text or is it a bug? 

We are working with SAP FIORI FOR SAP S/4HANA 2022 SP1. 

Thank you for your help. David

entry.title: ILM Compatibility of '/opt/doc SAP Object for Archiving
entry.content:
We are currently reviewing our document archiving strategy and need clarification on the SAP object `/opt/doc`. Specifically, we want to determine whether this object is compatible with SAP Information Lifecycle Management (ILM) for archiving and retention management, or if it only supports classical archiving. During our investigation, we noticed that `/opt/doc` is not present in the ILM Archive Repository Analysis tool (ILMARA), which raises concerns about its ILM readiness. 

Whether `/opt/doc` is an ILM-compatible archiving object or a classical archiving object.

If it is classical only, what are the best practices or recommendations for migrating to ILM or managing lifecycle policies for these documents?

entry.title: Preventing closing the Job Requisition until all candidates are in the final Talent Pipeline status
entry.content:
Hello,

We are facing a situation where recruiters are closing job requisitions without moving all candidates to their final Talent Pipeline statuses. Specifically, not-selected candidates are not being moved to the "Rejected" status.

We are aware about the functionality to automatically move candidates to "Requisition Closed" status, but that"s not how we have configured our Talent Pipeline. We have 2 sets of "Rejected" statuses: one that automatically sends out the Rejection Email and one that does not send out Rejection Email (in case candidate received an individual feedback or too much time has passed between the application submition and rejection).

Is there a way for SuccessFactors to prevent the closure of a job requisition until all candidates have been moved to their final Talent Pipeline statuses (with our setup).

Best regards,

Radek

entry.title: Re: Tips & Tricks for the new SAP Community Site
entry.content: ha Thank you so much Ivan. You have posted a very useful tip. It has been very frustrating to navigate from the home page to Technology Q&A section and then realizing the Q&A are not filtered by topic. Following your tip, I have now saved the link for SAP Analytics Cloud topics.  Cheers Vijay Nandipati.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA BP screen enhancement with custom tab
entry.content:
Hi Andi,

Thanks for your timely response. The issue actually is that i only want to save update user and date for current active tab.

  1. open a tab,
  2. click save button,
  3. the update user and date should be saved into database for current active tab.

however, BDT framework will process all the PBO and PAI modules after saving which leads to the update user and date within the other two tabs even both are not active will be updated as well.
I tried to set a flag in PBO and in PAI for each tab page, only the flag equals true then set the value for update user and date. but it didn't work.

of course, those six customized fields name are different and every two of them have been assigned to each tab page. all those fields are appended LFA1.

I hope it is clear for the problem. if not, you can ping me by teams "walter.wu@sap.com".

Thanks a lot in advance!

Try to resume aborted transports etc.

entry.title: Re: I have a query in the SAP SAC, does it support Table...
entry.content:
Hi Martin

I have a few questions regarding the limitations of SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) when it comes to replicating Webi Trellis  vertical /horizontal Tables.

1) Does SAC support cell level calculations similar to the structure in Webi?
2) What functions in Webi, such as force merge merge dimension  and where, have equivalent functions in SAC, and what are those replacements?
3) Does SAC support publication formats like PDF, HTML, and Excel?

Thanks in Advance for your assistance

entry.title: Re: Close Period (MMPV) Not the current calendar year
entry.content:
Hello

fill the new period data, press "ENTER" on the keyboard and then button "Run". Do it with every period till you get to the period that you want.

Best Regards 

Ghazal Afshar

entry.title: Re: How sovanta uses Joule Studio to take ticketing systems to the next level
entry.content:
I really like the use case "ticketing" you described. It is understandable for almost everybody, because we all had the experience to at least write a ticket and try to get an answer. However, what I miss is the SAP context. You are using Joule and you write:

...

The combination of generative AI and deep integration into SAP systems already makes Joule a powerful tool for our clients.

...

So where in your use case is the deep integration into an SAP system? I understand that your ticketing system is on JIRA, right? And you connected it to the external RAG.

It would be interesting to conntect a SAP Service Cloud to this ticketing process, for example.

entry.title: Re: I have a query in the SAP SAC, does it support Table...
entry.content:
Hello @ujjani.
I'm sorry, but I cannot answer questions regarding Webi, since I have no expierence in this field.
I can tell you that a SAC table can handle cell level calculations of BW Queries (as live model)... but you cannot create cell level calculations in SAC out of an import model.
Regarding the publication formats, check this SAP help link:
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/86c2d7d88b134e45907453612c4f559a.html
BR, Martin

entry.title: Mastering Executive Communication: Crafting Messages That Drive Action
entry.content:
Be Clear and Concise

Executives appreciate brevity. Get straight to the point and avoid unnecessary details. Use clear and straightforward language to convey your message. This not only respects their time but also ensures that your key points are easily understood.

Focus on the Big Picture

Executives are strategic thinkers. Frame your message in the context of the organization’s goals and objectives. Highlight how your proposal or information aligns with the company’s vision and contributes to its success. This helps executives see the relevance and importance of your message.

Provide Data and Evidence

Support your message with relevant data and evidence. Executives rely on facts and figures to make informed decisions. Present your data in a clear and accessible format, such as charts or bullet points, to make it easy to digest.

Offer Solutions, Not Just Problems

When presenting an issue, always accompany it with potential solutions. Executives are problem-solvers and appreciate proactive thinking. Outline the steps you recommend and explain the benefits and potential outcomes. This shows that you’ve thought through the issue and are ready to take action.

Be Prepared for Questions

Anticipate the questions executives might have and be prepared with answers. This demonstrates your thorough understanding of the topic and your readiness to engage in a meaningful discussion. It also helps in addressing any concerns they might have promptly.

Use Visual Aids

Visual aids can enhance your message and make it more memorable. Use slides, charts, or infographics to highlight key points and data. Visuals can help in breaking down complex information and making it more accessible.

Follow Up

After your initial communication, follow up with a summary of the key points and any agreed-upon actions. This reinforces your message and ensures that everyone is on the same page. It also shows your commitment to seeing the issue through to resolution.

Conclusion

Effective communication with executives is about clarity, relevance, and actionability. By being concise, focusing on the big picture, providing data, offering solutions, being prepared for questions, using visual aids, and following up, you can ensure your messages are well-received and acted upon. Master these techniques to enhance your executive communication skills and drive organizational success.

entry.title: Re: Introducing the new pipeline concept in Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hey Alex,

Great blogs on Pipeline and thanks for keeping us updated with new features of this concept.

I have seen that in generic step 02 and 06 the payload is stored as a property for further error handling and storing in JMS.  We are a bit skeptical about this and would like to know if you guys have tested for high volume high load (not 50 MB huge, but few MBs) error scenarios to observe the processing of the payload as a property.

Sagar

entry.title: Re: Setup: Only send payment advice via mail if supplier was paid more than 4 invoices
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Blog it forward - third time's a charm
entry.content:
I attended SAP Insider in Las Vegas this year, and I was asked why I haven't blogged for a while.  Well a few reasons - I had an injury that stopped me from writing (now fully recovered) and I was super busy on an Ariba project that included weekend work.

Now on to more fun things:

I am still ASUG chapter chair for the Mid-Atlantic region.  We are seeking speakers and sponsors for our upcoming events in September (Richmond, VA) and December in Baltimore.  This year ASUG honored me as volunteer of the year.

In my spare time, I still like to walk and crochet.   I finished a 3 year stint as a Finance volunteer for my local PMI Washington DC chapter; we left the chapter in great financial shape. 

Since the last blog-it-forward, we have upgraded to S/4HANA 2022 and implemented SAP Service Cloud (in addition to Ariba).  At work I support the finance areas, security, Solution Manager, now Ariba and other projects.  It's never a dull moment at work.

In terms of SAP TechEd, I miss the in person "SAP Community Family Reunion" as Mark Yolton called it.  I found this article that ASUG wrote about it and it's all still relevant today.  During COVID times, I joined TechEd remotely - you can see the YouTube here: 

I also enjoyed the virtual TechEds remotely in Europe or India, and many times woke up early to catch the keynotes.

Now on to some non-SAP topics - famous people I've seen or met:

1. Connie Chung and Maury Povich - they were on my very first flight to New York City

2. Former speaker of the house Newt Gingrich - seen him several times at old National Airport.  I last saw him across the aisle on a plane when I flew back from ASUG Chicago a few years ago.

3. Senator Bob Dole - my first job in a nursing home as a teenager he visited the place no less than 3 times.

4. Supreme Court Justice Anthony Kennedy - he sat next to me on a flight from San Francisco to Washington Dulles.  He was a gentleman who offered to help me with my luggage.

5. Michael Strahan - I met him in person at ASUG Annual Conference, thanks to ASUG

6. Steve Wozniak - founder of Apple - I met him at ASUG Annual Conference

7. Maurice DuBois - CBS News co-anchor - I've seen him several times on the street when I was working in New York City

8. On many flights from Washington DC to Kansas City (or visa-versa) I've seen the Governor of Missouri, US Senators from Kansas, and local Kansas congressmen.

9. Gloria Loring - she sang and wrote the song for "Facts of Life" and was an actress on Days of Our Lives - she was on my flight to Chicago from Washington, DC

10. Susan Lucci  - she was on my flight from Washington Reagan to New York LaGuardia

Favorite TV Shows by Decade:

1. 60's - Bewitched

2. 70's Mary Tyler Moore Show

3. 80's - couldn't think of one; Cheers, maybe?  I remember Woody asking Norm how his day was going and he said "Woody, it's a dog-eat-dog world and I'm wearing Milk-Bone dog underwear".

4. 90's Seinfeld

5. 2000's - Gilmore Girls

6. 2010 - Modern Family

What are your favorite shows?

Please access our Frequently asked questions about the redesigned SAP Learning Hub, partner edition. Download the Document
more for details.
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: In sourcing and procurement, Output determination for...
entry.content: First create Form template ,then BRF Plus.....Under message output ....Please complete all configurations related to assign form. Then there is an app called "output parameter determination", Please define medium etc.

entry.title: Preparations for setting up integration between S/4HANA Private Cloud and Concur
entry.content:
The standard SAP Integration with Concur Solutions (SAP ICS) makes it really easy to quickly setup and configure an integration between your S/4HANA Private Cloud system and SAP Concur. Our Concur Service Delivery team have helped many clients in getting this setup, but there are certain issues we often see clients run into. Fortunately, there are a few easy checks and guidelines you can follow to avoid them.

The purpose of this article is to give a quick and simple overview of the guidelines to ensure you are best prepared for a smooth integration. 

Integration:

 For integration with S/4HANA Private Cloud version 2020 and higher, it is crucial that you select the documentation on the SAP Help Portal according to the version you are using (see screenshot below).

Starting from SAP S/4HANA 2020, your system will automatically receive the latest software version, as the integration function is delivered as a core feature and no longer as an add-on.

Authorizations and AuthProfiles should be assigned according to the Master Guide (again make sure you’ve selected your version).

Further information about your version can be found in SAP Note 2976103 .

Importing Certificates:

Unlock the firewall and import the certificates in STRUST under SSL Client Standard.

  • DigiCert Global Root CA
  • DigiCert Global Root G2
  • DigiCert Global Root G3

The certificates can be obtained from the DigiCert website:
www.digicert.com/digicert-root-certificates.htm

Enable Communication (Firewall):

The following addresses must be allowed in the firewall for outbound communication via REST API:

EMEA datacenter

  • eu2.concursolutions.com
  • eu2.api.concursolutions.com

US datacenter

  • us2.concursolutions.com
  • us2.api.concursolutions.com

Detailed information on this, as well as on integration via middleware (if required), can be found in SAP Note 2914977 - FAQ: Concur Certificates, Authentication, and Connectivity.

For customers with SAP integration platforms, we recommend the following documentation in the SAP Business Accelerator:  https://api.sap.com/package/ConcurIntegrationDataExportandFIPosting/integrationflow

Next Steps:

  • Review the settings of the Crypto Library and Cipher Suite (SAP Note  2914977)

entry.title: Re: High-Impact Use Cases of SAP Joule AI Agents
entry.content:
Can you choose some inventory use cases to create some agentic ai use cases ?
this is where i'm struggling as i'm trying to find solution for this can you help me in this

entry.title: Re: In sourcing and procurement, Output determination for...
entry.content: Hi, Internal Request, output parameter is already defined and working fine, my query is whether we can configure it for Quotation created with reference to RFQ?

entry.title: Re: CDS : How to define Hierarchy View
entry.content:
Hi Tao,

Yes you need to define association and also expose it explicitly. 

following are the View you asked for

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZOQ_CONNID_NM'

@EndUserText.label: 'Connection Hierarchy NodeNames'

@analytics : { dataCategory: #DIMENSION, dataExtraction.enabled: true }

@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'nodeName'

 

define view ZOQ_CONNECTION_NODENAME as select from zoq_connid_hd

association [1] to ZOQ_CONNECTION_HIERARCHY_DIR as _dir on $projection.hieid = _dir.hieid

{

@ObjectModel.foreignKey.association: '_dir'

key hieid,

@ObjectModel.text.element: 'nodetxt'

key hieid as nodeName,

 

@Semantics.text: true

hiertxt as nodetxt,

_dir

}

 

 

 

 

@AbapCatalog.sqlViewName: 'ZOQ_AIRLINE1'

@EndUserText.label: 'Airline'

@analytics : { dataCategory: #DIMENSION, dataExtraction.enabled: true }

 

@ObjectModel.representativeKey: 'carrid'

define view ZOQ_AIRLINE as select

from zoq_scarr

 

/* association to zsu_airline_text as _text on

$projection.carrid = _text.carrid*/

 

{

@ObjectModel.text.element: 'txtsh'

key carrid,

@APIElement.state: #DEPRECATED

@APIElement.successor: ['currcodenew' ]

currcode,

// @EndUserText.label: 'Attribute'

// currcode as attr,

 

currcode as currcodeNew,

carrname,

 

@Semantics.text: true

zoq_scarr.carrname as txtsh,

url

// _text

}

 

 

ZLR_CONN is mentioned above in comment. Hope it helps.

 

 

Kind Regards,

Lina

entry.title: Preparations for setting up integration between S/4HANA Public Cloud and Concur
entry.content:
The standard SAP Integration with Concur Solutions (SAP ICS) makes it really easy to quickly setup and configure an integration between your S/4HANA Public Cloud system and SAP Concur. Our Concur Service Delivery team have helped many clients in getting this setup, but there are certain issues we often see clients run into. For S/4HANA Public Cloud the integration is available by default, but there are still a few things you can check upfront. 

The purpose of this article is to give a quick and simple overview of the guidelines to ensure you are best prepared for a smooth integration. 

Integration:

Your system will automatically receive the latest software version, as the integration function is delivered as a core feature and no longer as an add-on.

Authorizations and AuthProfiles should be assigned according to the Master Guide.

For SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud systems, SAP Concur is available as a standardized catalog integration, without the need for additional add-on installation. It will be automatically updated along with your cloud system.

Next Steps:

As a basis for activation and information on what is automatically carried out in the background, we recommend the following webseite, which describes the integration and all related processes for cloud systems.

The aforementioned website is also linked on the main SAP Help page for S/4HANA Cloud.:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD_INTEGRATION_WITH_CONCUR_SOLUTIONS

entry.title: Re: introdution of my self
entry.content:
Hi! welcome to the SAP Community^^

Where are you based in? Maybe there's a location group that you can join to interact, learn and have a good time.

You can check our managed tags page as well and follow the topics that you are interested in.

entry.title: How can I remove the branch code from the sales order?
entry.content:
How can I remove the branch code from the sales order? I don't need this field and I can't find where to set it.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How can I remove the branch code from the sales order?
entry.content:
Why This Happens?

The “Branch Code” field is standard SAP behavior for company codes based in Thailand. This requirement is enforced by SAP due to Thai VAT regulations and legal reporting obligations. SAP Notes such as 1897424 and 2115058 confirm this.

As such, SAP does not support disabling or removing this field for Thai-based entities. It is hardcoded into the system to ensure compliance with local tax laws.

 

Recommended Workaround

If your business scenario does not require active use of the Branch Code field for each order:

  1. Default a Branch Code at the Business Partner Level:
    You can assign a default branch code (e.g., 00000) directly in the customer master (Business Partner record). This will auto-populate the field in the sales order and eliminate the need for manual entry.

Branch Code Assignments Help Portal

 

I hope this helps!

entry.title: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud: Installation of Intelligent Applications and Data Packages
entry.content:
As a continuation of the series of the Introductory Guide to Provisioning of SAP Business Data Cloud and its various components, this blog will help you install intelligent applications within Business Data Cloud. Please refer to the different parts to understand the entire process holistically.

Part 1: Provisioning of Business Data Cloud through SAP4Me
Part 2: Provisioning of SAP Datasphere
Part 3: Provisioning of SAP Analytics Cloud
Part 4: Provisioning of SAP Databricks
Part 5: Installation of Intelligent Apps and Data Packages

#Part 5

In this blog, we will discuss in detail how to install the Intelligent Application from the Business Data Cloud Cockpit.

Installation Process

  1. Confirm that you have the BDC Administrator role, which is necessary to install and manage intelligent apps
  2. Access the installation settings through SAP Business Data Cloud
  3. Choose the intelligent apps eligible for installation based on your entitlements
  4. Proceed with the installation, following any on-screen instructions or prompts
  5. Manage installed intelligent apps within SAP Business Data Cloud. Regularly check for updates or enhancements provided by SAP

Additional Information

  • Note that intelligent apps are SAP-managed
  • Only SAP staff can create, update, and manage the lifecycle of these applications. Ensure you stay updated with any changes delivered by SAP
Step-by-step guide to Install Intelligent Applications:

An Intelligent app in 'Integration with Business Data Cloud' can only be installed once. After installation, its status no longer displays "Available"

  1. Launch SAP Business Data Cloud on your machine
  2. Navigate to "Intelligent apps and Data Packages" in the side menu
  3. Select the "Available" tab to see the list of intelligent apps that can be installed. Pick the one you wish to install

    Image 1
  4. Verify that the selected app has no warning symbol in the Minimum System Version column. If a warning symbol appears, installation is not possible. Consult "Minimum System Version Incompatibility with intelligent apps" for additional information
  5. On the app's page, please go over the details to ensure it's the app you want to install
  6. Click on "Install" located in the top-right corner of the app's page

    Image 2
  7. In the Install Options dialog, select the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system that will supply the business data
  8. Only select systems compatible with the app's minimum version requirements will be available
  9. Pick the formation from the Install Location dropdown where the insight app should be installed
  10. Formation options are restricted to those including the chosen source system
  11. Click "Install" to continue
  12. The status of the insight app updates to "Installing"

    Image 3
  13. Once the Intelligent app has been installed, it moves to the Installed status (see Reviewing Installed intelligent apps)
  14. Once the insight app has been installed, you can view it in SAP Business Data Cloud (see Viewing Intelligent apps)

    Image 4
  15. Now, to launch the system, just click on the installed version
  16. Click on the 'Open App' section

    Image 5
  17. By following these structured steps, you can efficiently install intelligent apps within SAP Business Data Cloud, ensuring seamless integration and data consumption through your system


Step-by-step guide to Install Data Packages:

A data package is eligible for activation if your global account has a commercial entitlement to the line of business (LoB) application, along with add-on entitlement to the data package.

A package can be activated only once in the SAP Datasphere Catalog. Once activated, the Available status no longer appears next to the package's name.

  1. Open SAP Business Data Cloud Cockpit
  2. Choose intelligent apps and Data Packages in the side navigation area
  3. Open the Available tab
    Image 6
  4. Choose the data package that you wish to activate
  5. As it opens, review the package's documentation and details to make sure this is the package you wish to activate
  6. Click Activate at the top-right corner of the page
    Image 7
  7. The Activation Options dialog opens. Specify the source SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition system that provides the business data
  8. Click Activate
  9. Once activated, the status will change
  10. Communicate with the SAP Datasphere administrator to authorize spaces that need to consume the data products included in the data package
  11. The Data Packages are activated from BDC Cockpit ; but you have to install the data products in SAP Datasphere. For, that you need to have the right set of roles and access rights in the datasphere tenant
  12. Let us now navigate to the SAP Datasphere tenant
  13. Navigate to 'Catalog and Marketplace'
    Image 9
  14. Search for the 'Data Product' from the search bar. Here, we have taken an example of the 'Company Code'

    Image 10
  15. Select the Data Product, and an overview will be displayed to you. Select the 'Install' button

    Image 11
  16.  You will then have to select the SAP-managed space where you would like to install the data product within SAP Datasphere
  17.  After that click on 'Start Import and Deploy'

With the installation and activation of the necessary components, including intelligent apps and their data products, you're now set to begin utilizing SAP Business Data Cloud effectively.

This blog series serves as a comprehensive guide to help you get started with BDC, ensuring that all processes are ready and operational for your use.

Happy Learning 
Shreyasri Bose

entry.title: Reclassification with "Regroup Receivables / Payables"
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm dealing with a subject I'm not familiar with : the reclassification of Accounts Payable (G/L account 40000000) to Accounts Payables for Fixed Asssets (G/L account 40500000)

I wonder if tile "Regroup Receivable / Payables" is the one that suites the best for this process or if it exists an other process/tile specific for Fixed Assets ?



What precautions should be taken before proceeding with the reclassification of Accounts Payable to Accounts Payables for Fixed Asssets ?

Thank you for your help ?

Erwan

entry.title: Re: AdobeSign Integration with Successfactors for Recruit...
entry.content: Hi Jisha, I have granted the API import and export permission but it still doesnt attach the document and status back to Successfactors.

entry.title: Collected information belongs to the topics of query execution and optimization in SAP HANA database
entry.content:
last updated: 2025-06-11

Introduction
There is huge knowledge available about SAP HANA query execution topic (incl. parsing / optimization / plans / engines / hints / etc.),  but the knowledge is very fragmented (Help Portal, Blog post, SAP KBA, SAP Note, SAP Learning, other). The aim of this article is simplify and make content consumption easier with a centralized page. There is no reason to duplicate the already well prepared content, so the goal is to collect the available knowledge and put it in a well structured format. The logic of the page follows the earlier published articles in the same series.

What is SAP HANA?

Table of Content (The internal links had been removed cause the new blogging platform only supports basic html, and the use of anchor links not supported.) 

  1. Definitions
  2. Where to find documentations in SAP Help Portal?
  3. Which are the master SAP Knowledge Base Articles?
  4. Which are the related SAP Knowledge Base Articles?
  5. Where to find learning materials?
  6. Concept of the SAP HANA query execution
  7. Which SAP HANA execution engines exist?
  8. Which SQL Statement Collection report can be used for monitoring query execution?
  9. Which other documents belongs to SAP HANA Execution Engine (HEX) and why is this particularly important?
  10. Which other documents belongs to hints?
  11. Which other documents belongs to plans?
  12. Other articles in this series
  13. Other articles in connected series
  14. Useful bookmark

Definitions
To put the topic in context we need to understand general terms and meanings correctly.

  • Query: A query is a question or inquiry about a set of data. We use Structured Query Language (SQL) to retrieve meaningful and relevant information from databases.
  • Query Compilation / "Query Preparation": The set of sequences to parse, check, optimize, and generate a plan is called query compilation.
  • Query Execution:  Query execution in a database involves a user submitting a query, which is then processed by the database engine to retrieve the requested data. This process includes several internal steps, such as parsing, optimization, and ultimately, the execution of a physical plan. 
  • Statement / Statement String / Statement Hash: 3588199 - Overview of the "statement string" / "statement hash" in the context of SAP HANA database?

IMPORTANT !
=================================================================

Where to find documentations in SAP Help Portal?

  • SAP HANA Performance Guide for Developers - SQL Process

Which are the master SAP Knowledge Base Articles?

  • 2124112 - FAQ: SAP HANA Parsing
  • 2000002 - FAQ: SAP HANA SQL Optimization

Which are the related SAP Knowledge Base Articles?

  • 2142945 - FAQ: SAP HANA Hints
  • 2400006 - FAQ: SAP HANA Statement Hints
  • 2570371 - FAQ: SAP HANA Execution Engine (HEX)
  • 2599949 - FAQ: SAP HANA Extended SQL Executor (ESX)

Where to find learning materials?

  • SAP Learning - A First Step Towards SAP HANA Query Optimization

=================================================================

Concept of the SAP HANA query execution
The below image shows the current SAP HANA query execution steps, logic in order. The following scenarios can occur:

  1. Plan cache entry not found
  2. Plan cache entry found

Image source:  SAP Learning - A First Step Towards SAP HANA Query Optimization

Which SAP HANA execution engines exist?

  • SAP HANA Performance Guide for Developers - Query Execution Engine Overview
  • 2000002 - FAQ: SAP HANA SQL Optimization - - > 37. Which SAP HANA execution engines exist?

Which SQL Statement Collection report can be used for monitoring query execution?

  • 1969700 - SQL Statement Collection for SAP HANA
  • 3327150 - SQL Statement Collection: "HANA_SQL_StatementHash_DataCollector" report for SAP HANA

Which other documents belongs to SAP HANA Execution Engine (HEX) and why is this particularly important?
HEX is a new SQL engine in HANA that is designed to replace other engines. The SQL optimizer in HANA prioritizes HEX plans over plans from old engines if the query can be executed by HEX. The optimizer does not directly compare HEX plans and old engine plans because HEX offers wider functionality and generally performs better with lower memory consumption. However, there may be cases where HEX shows worse performance.

  • 2847700 - How-to: Circumventing the HEX Engine in Specific Cases
  • 3326981 - Why HEX Plans fallback to old engines such as Join, OLAP, ESX

Which other documents belongs to hints?

  • 2700051 - Delivery of SAP HANA Statement Hints
  • 3079964 - When to apply hints and why are they needed
  • 3556465 - How to apply hint on statement level in SAP HANA?
  • 2509161 - Set SQL Execution Hints in the Graphical Calculation View on the Model Level
  • 3019087 - How to Apply HINTS / Calculation Engine Settings for Queries Against Calculation Views

Which other documents belongs to plans?

  • 2222321 - How-To: Pinning SAP HANA SQL Plans

Other articles in this series

✍️ Collected information about memory in context of SAP HANA
✍️ Collected information regarding partitioning in SAP HANA (with examples)
✍️ Collected information regarding High Availability and Disaster Recovery in SAP HANA
✍️ Collected information about reclaim / shrink / defragmentation topic in context of SAP HANA persiste...

Other articles in connected series

✍️ Where can I find knowledge and information belongs to SAP HANA?
✍️ Where can I find information about the available tools for SAP HANA (all types of use)?

Useful bookmark

🔖 2872774 – Bookmark of Frequently Asked Questions for SAP HANA
🔖 3311408 – Bookmark of SQL Statement Collection reports for SAP HANA
🔖 2658020 – List of SAP HANA SQL Error Codes

Release Information

Release DateDescription
2025.06.11First/initial Release of the SAP Blog Post documentation (Technical Article).

But EoD is not visible.

entry.title: Re: Incorrect delivery status "complete" after partial de...

You followed me via DM to help you and I gave the solution.  Either close this discussion by Accepting the Right Answer or update here still what issue you face.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting value should be added in material price while doing GRN
entry.content: Thanks for the quick reply.

entry.title: Re: Setup: Only send payment advice via mail if supplier...
entry.content:
Hi,

You can make the settings in the configuration activity Set Up Payment Methods per Company Code for Payment Transactions (101044). In the Payment Advice Note Control (Pyt Adv. Control) section, specify "restricted to 4 rows" in the "Note to payee lines on the form" field,  and select the "Payment advice after ... lines" option for "Payment advice output according to no. of lines". 

 

Best regards,

Sharon

entry.title: Re: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content: Hello Gabor, the reconciliation is only supported for non-posted bank statement flows. Once the bank statement is posted, the bank statement flows (source application BS) will be deleted and replaced by accounting flows (source application BSEGV).

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting value should be added in material price while doing GRN
entry.content: But can you pls guide me, how to set subcontracting charges as service item in PO? I have added first line with item category "L" with price 200 INR. But little confuse, how to add service item. Because in SAP S/4HANA public cloud, there is no "D" item category.

entry.title: Passed SAP Certified Associate - Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects Exam
entry.content: I'm happy to share that I’ve successfully passed the SAP Certified Associate - Managing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Projects exam. It was a challenging yet rewarding experience, and I’m excited to apply this knowledge in real-world projects. Grateful for the Passexam4sure learning journey!

entry.title: Re: How to map SAP ERP fields to SAP BW/4HANA InfoObjects?
entry.content:
Hi @hadi_jafari 

Thank you for your work. Can you send me this excel file to my e-mail address too?

jakaree.k@round2solutions.com


Regards

entry.title: Re: Discover the Fresh New Design of SAP Help Portal
entry.content:
The design looks good and fits into the current design language. But one question remains open: will there be a dark mode? A  lot of other documentations, especially technical ones, have the option to switch between dark and light mode. Would be great to see something similar!

Thanks a lot for the update!

entry.title: Re: Reclassification with "Regroup Receivables / Payables"
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

If you're trying to reclassify Accounts Payable (40000000) to Accounts Payable for Fixed Assets (40500000) for reporting purposes, the "Regroup Receivables / Payables" tile is not ideal. That app is meant for automatically netting customer/vendor balances, not for reclassifying payables based on the type of purchase (like fixed assets).

Instead, you should use:

  • "Post General Journal Entries": Manually move the balance from 40000000 to 40500000 with a clear reference to the asset or invoice.
  • Or, if available, "Reclassify Payables and Receivables": Offers more control and is better suited for reporting reclassifications.

 

Before proceeding, make sure to:

  1. Double-check the payable is actually for a fixed asset (linked to an asset invoice or PO).
  2. Document the entry well for audit purposes.
  3. Plan to reverse it next period if it's just for month-end reporting.
  4. Align with accounting policy ; some countries require this, others don’t.

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content: Hello Amy, can we summarize the flow somehow? First, there needs to be a bank statement created, but not posted? Then we would be able to see it in the 'Reconcile Cash Flow' app? Also, can you please expand BS (bank statement?) and BSEGV?.

entry.title: Re: Perform Manual Reconciliation
entry.content: Hello Gabor, yes you can only reconcile non-posted bank statements with forecasted cash flows.

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to download the DME tree
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition using CBC, you can’t directly download the DME tree (tags) from configuration activity 100558. Unfortunately, the export option (like .xmt files) isn’t available in the cloud version due to restricted backend access.

 

What you can do :

  • View and edit the DME tree via the CBC activity, but there’s no download button.
  • Manually copy the structure (e.g., to Excel) if needed for documentation.
  • If you really need an export (e.g., for reuse in another tenant), you can raise a support ticket with SAP and ask for the DME tree to be exported.

Note: The "Manage DME Trees" app with export functions exists only in on-premise or private cloud, not in public cloud.

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy
